mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer demand, which drives corporate earnings upward and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-trading-day horizon. This is an always-on trend signal that does not require a threshold trigger.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year Rank IC remains below 0.03 or the correlation between RSAFS growth and forward 20-day returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05) over the tested period.

mechanism_narrative: A downward crossing of the federal funds rate below 0.10% signals an aggressive easing stance by the Fed that compresses discount rates and boosts equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprice risk.
failure_condition: In instances where FEDFUNDS ≤ 0.10%, the average forward 5-day return is not significantly greater than zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) or the frequency of positive returns is ≤ 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in employment (PAYEMS) and reduction in unemployment (UNRATE) indicates a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns. The joint behavior of these two series amplifies the signal beyond single-series effects.
failure_condition: When PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls concurrently, the proportion of positive 20-day returns is ≤ 55% over any 3-year rolling window, or the combined signal does not outperform the sum of individual signals in a linear regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-unemployment regime (UNRATE > 5.0%), rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals a supply-side recovery that precedes earnings improvements, forecasting positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon. The signal is regime-conditional: it only activates when unemployment is elevated.
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE > 5.0% and INDPRO increases, the average 60-day forward return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the success ratio of positive returns is below 60%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals weakening labor demand, which reduces consumer income and spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-trading-day horizon as markets react to deterioration. This is a threshold-triggered signal activated when the monthly change in PAYEMS falls below -100.
candidate_trigger: monthly change in PAYEMS < -100 (i.e., loss of more than 100,000 jobs)
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, the average forward 5-day return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the proportion of negative returns is ≤ 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts (HOUST) below a threshold of 900 thousand units signals weakening residential investment, which drags on economic growth and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-trading-day horizon. The signal is threshold-triggered and becomes active when HOUST drops below 900.
failure_condition: When HOUST < 900, the average forward 20-day return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the proportion of negative returns is ≤ 55%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS ≤ 0.25%) and rising inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5% year-over-year) creates a negative real interest rate environment that erodes fixed-income appeal but may boost equity valuations in the short run due to the 'reach for yield' effect, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon. The interaction between monetary conditions and inflation is critical.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS ≤ 0.25% and CPIAUCSL year-over-year > 2.5%, the average 60-day forward return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the combined signal fails to outperform a simple inflation-only or rate-only signal in a predictive model.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime of elevated unemployment (UNRATE > 6.0%), a sharp increase in real retail sales (RSAFS > 430,000) signals a consumption-driven recovery that boosts corporate earnings and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-trading-day horizon. The regime condition must be active for the signal to be reliable.
failure_condition: When RSAFS > 430,000 and UNRATE > 6.0%, the average forward 5-day return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the success ratio is below 55%.
